title: Investment Memo Template
description: Defines the structure of a Nido Ventures investment memo. Each section maps to a primary call stage.

memo_version.description: Increments with each call (1 after Call 1, 2 after Call 2, 3 after Call 3)

section_definitions.title: Executive Summary
section_definitions.description: Deal terms, round structure, check size, valuation, and a concise overview of the opportunity. The ONLY section that may briefly touch all areas — keep it to a standalone summary for the IC. The rest of the memo elaborates; do not elaborate here.

section_definitions.title: Investment Thesis
section_definitions.description: 3-5 bullet points on WHY this is a compelling investment. ONLY reasoning: market timing, founder-market fit, unfair advantages, strategic positioning. Do NOT restate what the company does, the problem, or product features — those belong in their own sections.

section_definitions.title: Team & Founders
section_definitions.description: Founder backgrounds, relevant experience, founder-market fit, team composition, key hires needed, and advisory board. Do NOT discuss the product or business model here.

section_definitions.title: Problem Statement
section_definitions.description: ONLY the industry pain point, status quo, who suffers, and the cost of inaction. Do NOT describe the solution or product — that belongs in Product & Technology.

section_definitions.title: Product & Technology
section_definitions.description: ONLY what they built and HOW it works: key features, tech stack, architecture, defensibility/moats, AI/ML usage, integrations, roadmap. Do NOT restate the problem — reference it briefly if needed.

section_definitions.title: Business Model
section_definitions.description: ONLY how they make money: revenue model (SaaS, marketplace, transactional), pricing strategy, unit economics (CAC, LTV, gross margin, payback period), expansion revenue. Do NOT restate product features or traction numbers.

section_definitions.title: Market Analysis
section_definitions.description: ONLY market sizing: TAM/SAM/SOM with bottom-up analysis, market drivers, regulatory tailwinds/headwinds, geographic considerations. Do NOT repeat the problem statement or product description.

section_definitions.title: GTM Strategy
section_definitions.description: Ideal customer profile with reasoning, sales motion, sales cycle, deal size, champion and decision-maker profiles, acquisition channels, and willingness-to-pay evidence. Do NOT restate the product or business model.

section_definitions.title: Competitive Landscape
section_definitions.description: ONLY positioning vs. competitors: direct and indirect competitors, differentiation, right to win, win/loss analysis, sustainable advantages. Compare features directly — do NOT restate the full product description.

section_definitions.title: Traction & Metrics
section_definitions.description: ONLY concrete numbers and evidence of PMF: client list, ARR/MRR, growth rate, pipeline, key KPIs (CAC, LTV, churn, NRR). Do NOT re-explain the business model or product.

section_definitions.title: Financial Review
section_definitions.description: ONLY the financial model assessment: revenue projections, burn rate, runway, path to profitability, key assumptions. Do NOT restate traction metrics or business model mechanics.

section_definitions.title: Concerns & Challenges
section_definitions.description: Cross-cutting risks, red flags, open questions, and mitigation strategies. Synthesize NEW concerns not already flagged inline in other sections. If a risk was noted in a specific section, reference it briefly — do not repeat in full.

section_definitions.title: Scoring Rubric
section_definitions.description: Quantitative scoring (1-5) across dimensions: Team, Market, Product, Business Model, Traction, Competition. Brief rationale per score referencing evidence from the relevant section — do not restate section content.
